findings.evidence: `nl -ba code/TauCeti/Analysis/Semigroups/Generator.lean` shows `mem_domain_iff` at lines 155-159 and `mem_domain_iff_tendsto` at lines 169-173, both proving the same `x ∈ S.domain ↔ ∃ y, Filter.Tendsto ...` by `Iff.rfl`; line 339 uses the duplicate.
findings.fix: Remove `mem_domain_iff_tendsto` and update the use at line 339 to `S.mem_domain_iff _`; keep only one public iff lemma.
findings.issue: `StronglyContinuousSemigroup.mem_domain_iff_tendsto` duplicates `mem_domain_iff` with the identical statement, so the public API exposes two names for the same generator-domain criterion.

summary: The semigroup, growth-bound, generator-density, and resolvent results are stated at the general C₀ level with contraction wrappers derived afterward. One duplicated public domain iff still creates two API names for the same theorem.
